title: Bidens selenactis
intro: Bidens selenactis, also known as Blackseed Beggar's Lice or Yellow Beggar's Lice, is a perennial plant native to eastern Australia and New South Wales. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Bidens selenactis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Bidens selenactis is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and fever.
taxonomy: Bidens selenactis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the yellow biddies or yellow tops. This species is native to eastern Australia and is often found growing in dry, open areas. Bidens selenactis is typically identified by its bright yellow flowers and hairy stems, which can grow up to 40 cm in height. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Bidens selenactis is closely related to other plants in the same family, such as dandelions and sunflowers. The classification of Bidens selenactis is further divided into its genus and species levels, with Bidens being the genus and selenactis being the species.
uses: Bidens selenactis, also known as Blackseed or Black Jack, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and seeds are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant has also been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, it is believed to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against infections and cellular damage. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Bidens selenactis.
